title: crataegus monogyna subsp. heterophylla
intro: Crataegus monogyna subsp. heterophylla, commonly known as hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiotonic effects, making it a popular treatment for cardiovascular issues, such as high blood pressure, atherosclerosis, and heart failure. The health benefits of Crataegus monogyna subsp. heterophylla include improved heart health, reduced risk of cardiovascular disease, and potential anti-aging properties. The plant is often used to make tea, capsules, and tinctures, as well as being added to food and beverages, highlighting its versatility in herbal preparations.
